heroEyebrow: Helping you make sense of what feels overwhelming, one conversation at a time
heroTitle: Therapy that meets you where you are
heroLeadItems: Maybe you've been carrying everything on your own for so long that it feels normal.

You might look like you're managing on the outside while quietly struggling on the inside. Whether you're feeling anxious, overwhelmed, stuck, or simply exhausted from trying to keep it all together, you don't have to navigate it alone.
Therapy is a space where you can slow down, make sense of what's been weighing on you, and begin moving toward a life that feels more balanced, meaningful, and authentic.
aboutBody:
Reaching out for therapy isn't always easy. Whether you've been thinking about it for months or you've landed here after a particularly difficult day, you've already taken an important first step.

My goal is to create a space where you don't feel judged, rushed, or expected to have everything figured out. Together, we'll explore what's been weighing on you, understand the patterns keeping you stuck, and work toward meaningful, lasting change.

I believe therapy isn't about "fixing" you, because you aren't broken. It's about helping you better understand yourself so you can move through life with greater clarity, confidence, and self-compassion.
consultationTitle: Your healing journey starts with one conversation.
consultationBody: If you're wondering whether therapy is the right fit, I offer a free 15-minute consultation where we can talk about what's bringing you here, answer your questions, and see if we're a good fit.
aboutButtonText: Learn About My Approach and More

connectLinks.label: Read my article at First Session

servicesTitle: You don't have to struggle alone with...

concernAreas.title: Anxiety & Overthinking
concernAreas.summary: When your mind won't slow down and you're constantly worrying, second-guessing yourself, or expecting the worst.

concernAreas.title: Trauma & PTSD
concernAreas.summary: Helping you process painful experiences, reduce their impact, and regain a sense of safety.

concernAreas.title: Depression & Low Mood
concernAreas.summary: When it feels difficult to find motivation, joy, or hope, therapy can help you reconnect with yourself.

concernAreas.title: Relationship Challenges
concernAreas.summary: Whether you're struggling with communication, boundaries, conflict, or heartbreak, you don't have to navigate it alone.

concernAreas.title: Self-Esteem & Confidence
concernAreas.summary: Break free from self-doubt, people-pleasing, and the feeling that you're never enough.

concernAreas.title: Stress & Burnout
concernAreas.summary: When you're constantly carrying everyone else's needs and have forgotten how to take care of your own.

audienceTitle: Whatever brought you here, you're welcome here.
audienceIntro: Whether you're struggling with anxiety, navigating a difficult relationship, processing past experiences, or simply feeling overwhelmed by life, therapy offers a space to slow down, feel heard, and begin moving toward meaningful change.
audienceFooter: You don't need to have everything figured out before reaching out. Together, we'll work toward helping you feel more like yourself again.
workTogetherTitle: What It's Like to Work Together
workTogetherIntro: Starting therapy can feel intimidating, especially if it's your first time. My goal is to create a space where you feel heard, supported, and never judged. Together, we'll explore what's been weighing on you, uncover patterns that may be keeping you stuck, and work toward meaningful, lasting change, at a pace that feels right for you.

workTogetherFeatures.title: A Safe Space
workTogetherFeatures.summary: Feel heard without judgment or pressure.

workTogetherFeatures.title: Tailored to You
workTogetherFeatures.summary: Every therapy journey is unique. Together, we'll find an approach that fits your needs and goals.

workTogetherFeatures.title: Practical & Meaningful
workTogetherFeatures.summary: Leave each session with greater clarity, insight, and tools you can use in everyday life.

approachesIntro:
I believe there is no one-size-fits-all approach to therapy because no two people have the same story. That's why I tailor each session to your unique experiences, goals, and strengths.

Drawing from a range of evidence-based approaches, we'll work together to better understand your thoughts, emotions, and patterns while building practical tools that support lasting change.

approachMethods.title: Cognitive Behavioural Therapy
approachMethods.summary: Learn to recognize and change unhelpful thought patterns.

approachMethods.title: Internal Family Systems
approachMethods.summary: Understand the different parts of yourself with curiosity and compassion.

approachMethods.title: Brainspotting
approachMethods.summary: Process trauma and emotional distress through a brain-body approach.

approachMethods.title: Solution-Focused Therapy
approachMethods.summary: Build on your strengths and create practical steps toward your goals.

approachMethods.title: Mindfulness
approachMethods.summary: Develop greater awareness and reduce overwhelm.

approachesFooter: The goal isn't to fit you into one therapy model. It's to find the approach that best supports your unique journey toward healing and growth.
virtualTherapyIntro: Whether you're in Vancouver, Calgary, Toronto, Halifax, or anywhere in between, you can access secure virtual therapy from the comfort and privacy of your own space.

virtualTherapyBenefits.title: 100% Virtual
virtualTherapyBenefits.summary: Attend sessions from the comfort of your own space.

virtualTherapyBenefits.title: Secure & Confidential
virtualTherapyBenefits.summary: Your privacy is always protected through secure video sessions.

virtualTherapyBenefits.title: Flexible Scheduling
virtualTherapyBenefits.summary: Appointments designed to fit into your busy life.

virtualTherapyBenefits.title: Serving Clients Across Canada
virtualTherapyBenefits.summary: Virtual therapy that's accessible wherever you are.

virtualTherapyWhereBody: I provide virtual psychotherapy to clients across Canada. Services are offered in accordance with the regulations of the province or territory where you are physically located at the time of your appointment.

faqs.question: How do I know if therapy is right for me?
faqs.answer: You don't have to be in crisis to benefit from therapy. Whether you're feeling anxious, overwhelmed, stuck, navigating a difficult life transition, or simply looking to understand yourself better, therapy can provide a supportive space to help you move forward.

faqs.question: What can I expect during my first session?
faqs.answer: Our first session is a chance to get to know each other. We'll talk about what's bringing you to therapy, explore your goals, and discuss what you'd like to gain from our work together. There's no pressure to have everything figured out—we'll move at a pace that feels comfortable for you.

faqs.question: Do you offer a free consultation?
faqs.answer: Yes! I offer a complimentary 15-minute virtual consultation where we can discuss your concerns, answer any questions, and determine whether we're a good fit to work together.

faqs.question: How long is each therapy session?
faqs.answer: Individual therapy sessions are 50 minutes in length and are conducted virtually through a secure video platform.

faqs.question: Are therapy sessions confidential?
faqs.answer: Yes. Your privacy is very important. Everything discussed in therapy is kept confidential, with a few legal and ethical exceptions that will be reviewed during the informed consent process.

faqs.question: Do you provide virtual therapy?
faqs.answer: Yes! All sessions are offered virtually, allowing you to access therapy from the comfort of your own space.

faqs.question: Do you accept insurance?
faqs.answer: Many extended health benefit plans cover psychotherapy services. I recommend checking with your insurance provider to confirm your coverage for services provided by a Registered Psychotherapist (Qualifying). (Once you're approved by TELUS Health, GreenShield, etc., this answer can be updated.)

faqs.question: How often should I attend therapy?
faqs.answer: The frequency of therapy depends on your goals and needs. Many clients begin with weekly sessions and gradually reduce the frequency as they make progress.

faqs.question: What if I'm nervous about starting therapy?
faqs.answer: Feeling nervous is completely normal. Starting therapy can feel like a big step, especially if it's your first experience. My goal is to create a warm, supportive, and non-judgmental space where you can feel comfortable being yourself.

faqs.question: How do I know if we're a good fit?
faqs.answer: Finding the right therapist matters. That's why I offer a free 15-minute consultation. It's an opportunity for us to meet, discuss your goals, and determine whether working together feels like the right fit.

faqs.question: What if I don't know what to talk about?
faqs.answer: You don't need to come into therapy with all the answers. Many people start therapy simply knowing that something doesn't feel right. Together, we'll explore what's been on your mind and help make sense of it, one conversation at a time.
